key_insight: PICDiff stitches together independently trained diffusion models for molecular subsystems by enforcing explicit physical interactions during inference, enabling generative modeling of chemically complex systems.
engineering_relevance: This offers a practical route to simulate large molecules and multi-component materials by breaking them into simpler parts while preserving physically meaningful structure and thermodynamics.
method: Train separate diffusion models on smaller subsystems.
Couple subsystem trajectories during inference with bonded and nonbonded interactions.
Use the coupled sampler to generate conformations and estimate thermodynamic behavior.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports quantitative evaluation on peptide-polymer binding and polymer conformation sampling, showing the method can sample conformations and model thermodynamics.
watch_item: Performance depends on how well the subsystem models and interaction coupling capture cross-component physics; missed couplings could limit accuracy in more complex systems.
paper_title: Stitching Molecular Worlds Together with Physics-Coupled Diffusion Models
